description: SpyTag003 - 16-aa peptide (RGVPHIVMVDAYKRYK), third-generation reactive Spy peptide that forms a spontaneous, irreversible isopeptide bond to SpyCatcher003 at near-diffusion-limited speed (~5.5e5 M-1 s-1).

references.authors: Keeble AH, Turkki P, Stokes S, Khairil Anuar INA, Rahikainen R, Hytonen VP, Howarth M
references.title: Approaching infinite affinity through engineering of peptide-protein interaction
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2019)

references.authors: Zakeri B, Fierer JO, Celik E, Chittock EC, Schwarz-Linek U, Moy VT, Howarth M
references.title: Peptide tag forming a rapid covalent bond to a protein, through engineering a bacterial adhesin
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2012)

references.authors: Li L, Fierer JO, Rapoport TA, Howarth M
references.title: Structural analysis and optimization of the covalent association between SpyCatcher and a peptide tag

references.authors: Keeble AH, Banerjee A, Ferla MP, Reddington SC, Khairil Anuar INA, Howarth M
references.title: Evolving Accelerated Amidation by SpyTag/SpyCatcher to Analyze Membrane Dynamics

references.authors: Khairil Anuar INA, Banerjee A, Keeble AH, Carella A, Nikov GI, Howarth M
references.title: Spy&Go purification of SpyTag-proteins using pseudo-SpyCatcher to access an oligomerization toolbox
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2019)

references.authors: Tan TK, Rijal P, Rahikainen R, Keeble AH, et al., Howarth M, Townsend AR
references.title: A COVID-19 vaccine candidate using SpyCatcher multimerization of the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein receptor-binding domain induces potent neutralising antibody responses
Power to the protein: enhancing and combining activities using the Spy toolbox

label: SpyTag003
note: 16-aa third-generation Spy peptide tag (RGVPHIVMVDAYKRYK). Engineered from SpyTag002 by appending an N-terminal Arg-Gly (RG) dipeptide and introducing the T112H mutation (CnaB2 numbering), descended from the CnaB2 domain of Streptococcus pyogenes FbaB. On mixing with its 116-aa partner SpyCatcher003 it forms a spontaneous, irreversible intermolecular isopeptide (amide) bond between its reactive Asp (CnaB2 Asp117) and Lys31 of SpyCatcher003, catalysed by SpyCatcher Glu77 - reaction approaches the diffusion limit. Fusable at N-terminus, C-terminus or internally. Contains no Cys.

provenance.sequence_source: QGX07220.1 (SpyTag003-MBP, synthetic construct; Keeble et al. 2019, PNAS, PMID 31822621, DOI 10.1073/pnas.1909653116), SpyTag003 domain at residues 20-35, 100% identity over all 16 aa; independently verified by fetching QGX07220.1 from NCBI protein and confirming RGVPHIVMVDAYKRYK at annotated feature 'SpyTag003 peptide' (Region 20..35)

functional_claims.label: Spontaneous, irreversible isopeptide bond to SpyCatcher003 approaching the diffusion limit

value.mechanism: intermolecular isopeptide (amide) bond formation on reconstitution of a split CnaB2-derived domain
value.reversibility: irreversible; no detectable side reactions and no self-reactivity

source.quote: Amide bond formation between an amine and a carboxylic acid here occurs without any activating groups, and half-time for reaction is less than 30 s with 10 microM of each partner... Other important features of our reactive pair include the absence of Cys in either partner, lack of side reactions, and specificity in a range of cellular systems.

functional_claims.label: Covalent (effectively infinite affinity); non-reactive SpyTag003 DA binds SpyCatcher003 with Kd ~21 nM

value.method: surface plasmon resonance on non-reactive SpyTag003 DA (D-to-A) variant; isotherm too tight to measure by ITC
value.note: the reactive SpyTag003/SpyCatcher003 pair forms a covalent bond (no equilibrium dissociation); 21 nM is the noncovalent docking affinity of the reaction-dead DA variant

source.quote: SpyTag003 DA:SpyCatcher003 still represents a high-affinity interaction: its affinity was too strong to measure by ITC, so we used surface plasmon resonance (SPR) to obtain a Kd = 21 +/- 4 nM
source.section: Results - Intracellular Coupling of Split Talin Fragments

functional_claims.label: Second-order rate constant 5.5e5 M-1 s-1 (~400-fold over SpyTag, 28-fold over SpyTag002)

value.reaction: covalent isopeptide bond formation with SpyCatcher003
value.interpretation: within the 10^5 to 10^6 M-1 s-1 onset of diffusion-controlled protein-protein interaction

source.quote: The second-order rate constant for SpyTag003/SpyCatcher003 was 5.5 +/- 0.6 x 10[5] M[-1]s[-1] (mean +/- 1 SD, n = 3), ~400-fold faster than the 1.4 x 10[3] M[-1]s[-1] previously shown for SpyTag/SpyCatcher, and 28-fold faster than the 2.0 x 10[4] M[-1]s[-1] for SpyTag002/SpyCatcher002... SpyTag003/SpyCatcher003 reaction went to >90% completion in 15 min
source.section: Results - Engineering Toward Diffusion-Controlled Protein Coupling

functional_claims.label: Reaction reaches >99% completion when one partner is in excess

value.condition: one partner in small excess, 1.5 h, 25 C, pH 7.0

source.quote: This analysis showed >99% completion for SpyTag003 with SpyCatcher003 in excess. Similarly, we found >99% completion for SpyCatcher003 with SpyTag003 in excess.
source.section: Results - Analysis of the SpyTag003/SpyCatcher003 System

functional_claims.label: Spy&Go (immobilised non-reactive SpyDock) affinity purification of SpyTag003 fusions

value.basis: exploits the strong noncovalent SpyTag003:SpyDock interaction
value.elution_note: SpyTag003 binds SpyDock more tightly than earlier generations and reportedly needs harsher elution; the precise elution condition is described in the dedicated Spy&Go report (PMID 30988307) which was only available at abstract level

source.quote: We took advantage of the strong noncovalent interaction between SpyTag003 and a nonreactive SpyCatcher variant (SpyDock) for affinity purification using the Spy&Go system. This enabled purification of SpyTag003 fusions from cell lysate with 92% purity.
source.section: Results - Analysis of the SpyTag003/SpyCatcher003 System

functional_claims.label: Specific reaction/detection across diverse model organisms and on live mammalian cells

validated_in: human cells (Expi293, cell surface and cytoplasm)
additional model-organism lysates by Western blot
value.context: cell-surface labelling in 1 min on live mammalian cells; specific Western-blot detection with minimal cross-reactivity to endogenous proteins; intracellular split-protein (talin) reconstitution in mammalian cytoplasm

source.quote: We applied this reactive pair for specific labeling of a plasma membrane target in 1 min on live mammalian cells. Sensitive and specific detection was also confirmed by Western blot in a range of model organisms.

functional_claims.label: Reacts efficiently at low nanomolar concentration; functions intracellularly and on cell surfaces

value.low_concentration_performance: half-time <5 min with both partners ~10 nM
value.note: earlier Spy generations show broad tolerance of pH, temperature and buffer and versatile fusion sites; the 003 pair inherits this robustness

source.quote: even with each partner at a thousand-fold lower concentration, the reaction half-time is less than 5 min. Therefore, this covalent reactivity can be harnessed for the study of proteins over a range of cellular concentrations... Previous iterations have shown broad tolerance of reaction conditions and versatility in the sites of fusion.

functional_claims.label: Fusable at N-terminus, C-terminus or internally

value.note: the engineered N-terminal Arg-Gly extension is part of SpyTag003 and contributes to its reactivity; like earlier Spy peptides the tag tolerates terminal or internal fusion

source.quote: SpyTag could be fused at either terminus or internally and reacted specifically at the mammalian cell surface.

functional_claims.label: Third-generation tag: N-terminal RG dipeptide + T112H over SpyTag002; pairs with SpyCatcher003

value.back_compatible: reacts with earlier-generation SpyCatcher partners (back-compatible), though optimally paired with SpyCatcher003

source.quote: we made the T112H mutation, which enhanced reaction rate... Appending the RG dipeptide at the N terminus of the Tag also caused a substantial improvement in reaction rate, giving us SpyTag003... We further characterized that SpyTag003 and SpyCatcher003 were back-compatible with previous SpyTag/SpyCatcher generations.
source.section: Results - Engineering Toward Diffusion-Controlled Protein Coupling

functional_claims.label: Engineered descendant of the S. pyogenes FbaB CnaB2 domain (Spy lineage)

value.domain: CnaB2 (immunoglobulin-like adhesin domain with spontaneous Lys-Asp isopeptide bond)
value.design: library-based evolution + rational/computational design of the split CnaB2 (SpyTag/SpyCatcher) system through generations 001, 002, 003

source.quote: The fibronectin binding protein, FbaB, from [S. pyogenes] contains a CnaB2 adhesin domain. CnaB2 is stabilized by a spontaneous reaction of Lys and Asp side chains to form an isopeptide bond... We previously achieved genetically encoded and covalent peptide-protein interaction by splitting CnaB2 into the 13-residue SpyTag peptide and the 116-residue SpyCatcher protein.
source.section: Results - Engineering Toward Diffusion-Controlled Protein Coupling

functional_claims.label: Rapid covalent module for protein assembly, detection, anchoring, vaccines and split-protein reconstitution

applications: rapid, sensitive detection (flow cytometry, Western blot)
fast cell-surface labelling on live cells
Spy&Go affinity purification of SpyTag003 fusions

source.quote: This work also supports the use of SpyTag003 to enhance the range of applications of a fused protein. Proteins linked to SpyTag and its variants are empowered for Spy&Go affinity purification, irreversible anchoring (on magnetic beads or biomaterials), or oligomerization (on coiled-coils or vaccine scaffolds).
